hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DIGS
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said:  
“Whoever extends a water spout, a drain, drives a peg, ties an animal, or digs a well in 
the path of Muslims and it causes harm or damage to anything, then he is liable for it."

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 49, H. 5343
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DIGS
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DIGS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٥٣٤٣

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DIGS A WELL OR SOMETHING ELSE IN HIS OWN PROPERTY OR I
hadiths.ar:
5345   -  وَّ رَّوَّى وُّهَّي ْث ُّ ن ْن ُّ جَّق ْصٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ ع ُّلا مٍ ذَّج َّلَّ ذَّإرَّ  
ق َّوْمٍ ي َّلْعَّث ُّ ق َّوَّق َّغَّ ق ِى  ن ِن ْزِهِمْ إ َّ ي َّص ْمَّن ُّون َّ ق َّال مِن ُّوإ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Hilal narrated from Uqbah ibn Khalid, from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"Among the rulings of the Prophet {saws} (peace upon him and his family) was:  
'There is no liability (Jubar) for injuries caused by a mine, a well, or an animal.'"  
[AL SADUQ] 
The term Ajma’ refers to domesticated animals from livestock, and Jubar means 
something that is considered accidental and does not entail financial compensation.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.154 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.827  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 49, H. 5344

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DIGS A WELL OR SOMETHING ELSE IN HIS OWN PROPERTY OR I
hadiths.ar: 5346  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّعِي ذٍ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إَّلن  ُّعْمَّان ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلص َّن  َّاحِ إَّلْكِن َّان ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو ع ن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ
hadiths.en:
Wuhayb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah 
{a.s}, who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a boy who entered the house of some people to 
play and fell into their well. Are they liable?  
Imam {a.s} said: “They are not liable, but if they are accused (of negligence or 
wrongdoing), then they are liable." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.154 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.212 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 
p.825 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.255  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« :

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 49, H. 5345

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DIGS A WELL OR SOMETHING ELSE IN HIS OWN PROPERTY OR I
hadiths.en:
Al-Husayn ibn Said narrated from Ali ibn Al -Numan, who narrated from Abu 
Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani, who narrates that Abu Abdullah {a.s} said:  
“Whoever causes harm to any part of the path of the Muslims is liable for it."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.350 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.155 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.230 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.231 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.825 • Al-Wafi, Vol.18 p.1072 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.19 p.238 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.241 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.243 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, 
Vol.2 p.535

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 49, H. 5346

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON REPORTS REGARDING ONE WHO DIGS A WELL OR SOMETHING ELSE IN HIS OWN PROPERTY OR I
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from Al -Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} that 
he was asked about an object placed on the road, and an animal passes by it, 
becomes startled, and injures its rider.  
Imam {a.s} said: “Anything that causes harm on the path of the Muslims, its owner is 
liable for whatever results from it." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 7 p.349 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.155 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 16 p.824 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.243

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 49, H. 5347

hadiths.chapter: 50. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
hadiths.ar:
5349  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ رِن َّاب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ ع   ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ جَّمَّلَّ عَّن ْذَّةُّ عَّلَّى ذَّإن  َّه ٍ ق َّوَّطِن َّث ْ 
رَّج ُّلًا ق َّق َّالَّ إلْع ُّزْمُّ عَّلَّى مَّوْلَّأةُّ .
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from Al -Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} that 
he was asked about a man passing through one of the paths of the Muslims, and his 
animal strikes a person with its leg.  
Imam {a.s} said: “He is not liable for what it strikes with its leg, but he is liable for what it 
strikes with its front legs because its hind leg is behind him if he is riding. However, if 
he is leading the animal, then by the permission of Allah {SWT}, he has control over its 
front legs and can place them wherever he wishes." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.155 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.284 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.842 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.247  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 50, H. 5348
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٠، ح ٥٣٤٨

hadiths.chapter: 50. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
hadiths.ar:
5350  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ي ُّون ُّسُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ رَّق َّعَّهُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال عَّامِ لأَّ ي َّع ْزَّمُّ إ َّهْلُّهَّا 
س َّي ْن اً مَّا ذَّإمَّث ْ مُّزْسَّلَّه ً
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri’ab, who narrated from Abu 
Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Mu hammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} regarding a man who placed his 
servant on an animal, and the animal trampled a man.  
Imam {a.s} said: “The compensation is upon the master." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.155  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 50, H. 5349

hadiths.chapter: 50. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
hadiths.en:
Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated, raising it to Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"The owners of livestock are not liable for anything as long as the animals are left to 
roam freely." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.351 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.155 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.234 • Al -Istibsar, Vol.4 p.286 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.841 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.246 • Mustadrak Al -
Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.318 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.328  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 50, H. 5350

hadiths.chapter: 50. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
hadiths.ar:
5352  -  « :وْ ج َّزَّجَّي ْهُّ ق َّق َّص َّى 
ن ِالْع َّزَّإمَّه ِ ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلز َّذِي ق َّي ْن ِ ن ِالس َّوِي  َّه ِ
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Al-Sakuni:  
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} used to hold the one leading (the animal), the one 
driving (the animal), and the rider accountable." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.156 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.843 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.29 
p.249  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 50, H. 5351
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN

hadiths.chapter: 50. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
hadiths.ar:
5353  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ع ِي َّاب ِ ن ْن ِ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم لَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ص َّم َّن َّ 
صَّاجِث َّ إَّلذ َّإن  َّه ِ مَّا وَّطِن َّث ْ ن ِي َّذَّي ْهَّا وَّ مَّا ن َّق َّحَّث ْ ن ِزِج ْلَّي ْهَّا ق َّلاَّ ص َّمَّان َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ ِ لأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّص ْزِن َّهَّا إ ِن ْسَّان
hadiths.en:
"And the Commander of the Faithful {a.s} ruled regarding an animal carrying 
two riders, and the animal killed or injured a man.  
Imam {a.s} ruled that the compensation would be equally divided between the two 
riders." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.156  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 50, H. 5352

hadiths.chapter: 50. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY WHEN AN
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim from Imam Jafar ibn Mu hammad Al-
Sadiq {a.s} from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}: 
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} held the owner of the animal liable for what it 
trampled with its front legs and what it struck with its hind legs. However, there is no 
liability upon him unless a person strikes the animal."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.156 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.248

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 50, H. 5353

hadiths.chapter: 51. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, who narrated from 
Abu Maryam Al-Ansari, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir 
{a.s} regarding two men who together cut off the hand of another man.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If the victim wishes to cut off their hands, he must first pay them the 
compensation for one hand, which they will then divide between themselves, and 
afterward, he may cut off both of their hands.  
However, if he wishes, he may take the compensation for his hand from them. If he 
cuts off the hand of only one of them, then the one whose hand was not cut off must 
pay a quarter of the compensation to the one whose hand was cut off."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.284 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.156 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.240 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.620 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.186

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 51, H. 5354

hadiths.chapter: 52. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
5356  -  وَّ ق ِى  ن َّوَّإذِرِ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ إ َّن  َّ إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  مِن ْ ق َّطْغِ رَّإ ْسِ  
إَّلْحَّى
hadiths.en:
Al-Husayn ibn Khalid narrated from Abu Al -Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-
Kadhim {a.s}, who said: 
"The diyyah (blood money) for a fetus, if its mother is struck and it is miscarried before 
the soul is breathed into it, is one hundred dinars, and this belongs to its heirs.  
As for the diyyah of a deceased person whose head is severed and whose abdomen is 
cut open, it does not go to his heirs; rather, it belongs to him alone."  
I asked: "What is the difference between the two cases?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "The fetus represents a future life, and its benefit is anticipated. But the 
deceased has already passed away, and his benefit has ceased. Therefore, when he 
is mutilated after death, the diyyah for the mutilation is his alone, not for others. It is  
to be used to perform Hajj on his behalf or for acts of charity and other good deeds."  
I then asked: "What if a man enters to dig a grave to wash the deceased, but while 
digging, he loses focus, and his shovel slips from his hand and strikes the abdomen of 
the deceased, cutting it open? What is required of him?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "If it happened in this manner, it is considered unintentional (khata’), 
and he is required to perform kaffarah (expiation): to free a slave, or fast for two 
consecutive months, or feed sixty poor people, giving each one a mudd (a measure) 
according to the mudd of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his 
family." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.157  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 52, H. 5355

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO

hadiths.chapter: 52. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
5357 - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْكَّان َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّطَّغَّ رَّإ ْسَّ إ لْمَّي ِّث ِ ق َّال 
إَّلذِّي َّه ُّ لِأ َّن  َّ جُّزْمَّي َّهُّ مَّي ِّي اً كَّحُّزْمَّي ِهِ وَّ هُّوَّ جَّى  
ق َّالَّ مُّصَّن ِّف ُّ هَّذ َّإ إلْكِي َّاب ِ رَّجِمَّهُّ إلل َّهُّ هَّذ َّإن ِ إلْحَّذِي ي َّان ِ ع َّي ْزُّ مُّح ْي َّلِق َّي ْن ِ لِأ َّن  َّ كُّل َّ وَّإجِذٍ مِن ْهُّمَّا   ق ِى  جَّالٍ مَّي َّى ق َّطَّغَّ رَّج ُّل  رَّإ ْسَّ مَّي ِّث ٍ وَّ 
كَّان َّ مِم َّن ْ إ َّرَّإذَّ ق َّي ْلَّهُّ ق ِى  جَّي َّاي ِهِ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ إلذِّي َّه ُّ وَّ مَّي َّى لَّمْ ي ُّ زِذْ ق َّي ْلَّهُّ ق ِى  جَّي َّاي ِهِ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ مِان َّه ُّ ذِي ن َّارٍ ذِي َّه ُّ إلْح َّن ِي ن ِ .
hadiths.en:
In Nawadir of Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr, it is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "Beheading the dead is more severe than beheading the 
living." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.348 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.157 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.272 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.296 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.763 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.328  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 52, H. 5356

hadiths.chapter: 52. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
5358  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّمِي لَّه َّ عَّن ْ إ ِسْحَّاق َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ: ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ مَّي ِّث  ق ُّطِغَّ رَّإ ْسُّهُّ ق َّالَّ 
«  إ ِن ْ ق ُّطِعَّث ْ ي َّمِي ن ُّهُّ إ َّوْ س َّى ْء  مِن ْ ج َّوَّإرِجِهِ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ 
إَّلْأ َّرْس ُّ لِلْا ِمَّامِ
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Abdullah ibn Muskan from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, regarding a man who beheaded a dead person 
Imam {a.s} said: "The blood money is upon him because the sanctity of the deceased is 
like his sanctity when he was alive." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The author of this book, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, said: These two narrations 
are not contradictory because each pertains to a specific situation. If a man beheads 
a deceased person and was among those who intended to kill him during his lifetime, 
then the full blood money is upon him. However, if he did not intend to kill him during 
his lifetime, then he is liable to pay one hundred dinars, equivalent to the blood money 
of a fetus. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.348 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.157 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.273 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.273 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.297 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.297 • Awali Al-La’ali, 
Vol.2 p.367 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.653 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.762 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.764  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 52, H. 5357

hadiths.chapter: 52. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abu Jamilah, from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:  
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a deceased person whose head was cut off.  
Imam {a.s} said: “The blood money is upon the one who did it."  
I asked: “Who receives his blood money?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “The Imam, for this belongs to Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. And 
if his right hand or any of his limbs is cut off, the compensation (Al-arsh) is also due to 
the Imam." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.158 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.272 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.297 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.764 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.326

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 52, H. 5358
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO

hadiths.chapter: 53. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who narrated from 
Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who slapped another man 
on his face, and the slap caused the skin to turn black.  
Imam {a.s} said: “If the slap causes the skin to turn black, the compensation is six 
dinars. If it turns green, the compensation is three dinars. If it turns red, the 
compensation is one and a half dinars. For the same injuries on the body, the 
compensation is half of that amount." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.158 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.706

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 53, H. 5359
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٣، ح ٥٣٥٩

hadiths.chapter: 54. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
hadiths.en:
Al-Husayn ibn Khalid narrated from Abu Al -Hasan Al-Awwal {a.s} that he was 
asked about a man who approached another man while he was lying down. When he 
got on top of him, the man woke up and struck him with a severe blow, killing him.  
Imam {a.s} said: “There is neither blood money (compensation) for him nor retaliation 
(legal retribution)." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.293 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.158 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.815

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 54, H. 5360

hadiths.chapter: 55. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, who narrated 
from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"The Commander of the Faithful {a.s} ruled regarding the demolition of a wall in which 
three people participated. When it collapsed on one of them and caused his death, he 
held the remaining two responsible for his blood money because each one of them is 
accountable for his companion." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.159

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 55, H. 5361
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٥، ح ٥٣٦١

hadiths.chapter: 56. CHAPTER ON A MAN WHO IS KILLED WHILE HE HAS
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Aslam Al -Jabali narrated from Yunus ibn Abd Al -Rahman, 
who narrated from Abdullah ibn Muskan, who narrated from Abu Basir, who said:  
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who is killed while he has debt and possesses 
no wealth. Do his heirs have the right to pardon his killer while he has debt?  
Imam {a.s} said: “The creditors are the ones who have a claim against the killer. If his 
heirs pardon his killer, they become responsible for paying the debt to the creditors. If 
they do not pardon him, then they are not responsible."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.159 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.314 • Mustadrak Al-
Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.253

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 56, H. 5362
